purpose: Preserve user corrections from the long Codex Ratchet lego-scaling thread so fresh Codex terminals do not repeat the same Wizard/version/bounded-work/tool-integration failures.

forbidden: Do not save Codex general memory into system_v4/a2_state.
Do not write Codex memory into claude-memory or hermes-current without explicit authorization.
Do not treat this memory packet as project canon or A2 state.

critical_user_corrections.correction: The current Wizard packet is v4.2 at /Users/joshuaeisenhart/wiki/wizard/packet-v4-2-current, not v4.1.
critical_user_corrections.operational_effect: Fresh Codex sessions must load Wizard v4.2 MMMs, boot/runtime, council/member/child, conformance, adapter, and output-contract docs. Do not silently fall back to v4.1.

critical_user_corrections.correction: Bounded means local claim ceiling and not skipping ahead to the salient high-level claim. It does not mean low volume, tiny serial cleanup, or cautious under-exploration.
critical_user_corrections.operational_effect: Run many independent bounded packets in parallel when safe. Strict gates apply to admission and promotion, not to broad exploration before the gate.

critical_user_corrections.correction: The user repeatedly explained that legos/tool packets/couplings are bounded precisely so they can be run at mass scale and in parallel.
critical_user_corrections.operational_effect: Use rolling waves, Claude/Gemini/runners, and parallel packet fanout for independent tool-function, tool-lego, graveyard, and coupling work. Serialize only shared-state writes, queue mutation, git/index, admission, and commits.

critical_user_corrections.correction: Days of cleanup before basic tool integration was a process failure. Tools should have been integrated first or in parallel, with cleanup only as a blocking side lane.
critical_user_corrections.operational_effect: Prioritize tool-function receipts and tool-lego fit before more broad cleanup. Cleanup is allowed only when it directly blocks tool receipts, runner safety, or evidence truth.

critical_user_corrections.correction: Numpy is a classical tool. Numpy-only sims are classical_baseline, useful as classical baselines but not evidence for QIT/nonclassical engines.
critical_user_corrections.operational_effect: Do not promote numpy-only rows to canonical/nonclassical/bridge claims. Every lego can have classical numpy/scipy versions, but bridge and nonclassical-adjacent/full versions must be separately earned.

critical_user_corrections.topic: User goals were not followed tightly enough
critical_user_corrections.correction: Prior goals over-weighted cleanup, Wizard route truth, and report repair, while under-weighting the user main objective: finish tool integration and run bounded legos/couplings at scale.
critical_user_corrections.operational_effect: Fresh goals must force tool-integration execution, not stop at planning or safety cleanup.

critical_user_corrections.correction: Wizard is useful as a thin routing, MMM/context, anti-collapse, fanout, premortem, audit, and receipt harness. It is harmful when it becomes the work itself.
critical_user_corrections.operational_effect: Do not let Wizard receipts replace sim/tool receipts. Do not spend a session debugging Wizard unless it directly blocks fanout or receipt truth.

main_sequence: Containment/preflight: git status, no Playwright, no unintended Computer Use helpers, no admission bypass, no duplicate queue bases, runner-preflight green, dry-run runner green.
Build/refresh tool-function receipt matrix: tool -> exact function/API -> receipt -> classical/bridge/nonclassical-adjacent -> load-bearing/supportive/fail -> lego targets.
Run broad bounded tool packets. Numpy/scipy are classical baseline only. Prioritize PyTorch, PyG, QuTiP, Qiskit, Clifford, GUDHI, TopoNetX, XGI, rustworkx, z3, cvc5, sympy, geomstats.
Bind tools to legos through tool-lego fit receipts.
Only then expand allowed couplings: entropy/operator/topology/order variants, Rosetta continuation, Carnot/Szilard/I Ching, tiny Prime/QIT sidecar N=64 with baselines.
runtime_policy: Use GPT/Codex low mainly as controller.
Use Claude Bridge heavily: Sonnet high for implementation/scout/audit; Haiku high for inventory/counting/file checks; Opus for premortem/arbitration/final audit.
Use Gemini if available but do not block on it.
Use runners for sim execution where safe.

recent_repo_state_snapshot_from_thread.recommended_next_packet: Tool-integration execution wave, or source-truth review of FEP/Z3 rows before simple C4 cleanup. Do not continue cleanup as default main work.

anti_regression_rules: Do not answer as if the user has not already explained bounded parallel work; treat it as established doctrine.
Do not ask whether numpy is classical; it is classical for this project.
Do not confuse v4.1 and v4.2 Wizard packets.
Do not report Wizard process when the user asks about actual project/sim state.
Do not mark a goal complete just because a small cleanup packet passed.
Do not treat passing lint or refreshed reports as proof that tools are integrated.
Do not call partial Wizard execution FULL.
open_next_actions: Validate this memory packet and MANIFEST.json.
Use this packet in fresh Codex terminal initialization.
Run the next broad bounded tool-function integration wave with receipts.
Update the tool-function/tool-lego matrix with exact receipt paths.
